title: Project Scaffolding
description: Create the initial Python project structure with pyproject.toml (uv), server.py stub, and tests/ directory. Set up dependencies (mflux, fastmcp, mcp) and dev dependencies (pytest, pytest-asyncio). Verify the project can be synced with uv and pytest runs with zero errors.
acceptance: pyproject.toml exists with correct project metadata and all runtime + dev dependencies
server.py exists with a minimal FastMCP server that starts without errors
tests/ directory exists with conftest.py and at least one placeholder test
uv sync succeeds without errors
uv run pytest tests/ exits 0
uv run python server.py --help shows usage (or starts without crashing)

title: Lazy Model Cache
description: Implement a model cache module that lazily loads mflux models on first use and caches them in memory keyed by (model_name, quantize). This avoids the multi-second model load on every generation call. The cache should support all mflux model families and handle load errors gracefully.
acceptance: A model cache exists that stores loaded models keyed by (model_name, quantize) tuple
First call for a given (model, quantize) loads the model; subsequent calls return the cached instance
Cache supports at minimum Flux2Klein, Flux2KleinEdit, ZImageTurbo model families
Model load errors are caught and returned as descriptive error messages, not unhandled exceptions
Unit tests verify cache hit/miss behavior using mocked model classes
Unit tests verify error handling when model load fails

title: generate_image MCP Tool
description: Implement the generate_image MCP tool that performs text-to-image generation using the mflux Python API. The tool should accept prompt, model, width, height, steps, seed, and quantize parameters. It should use the lazy model cache, generate an image, and return it via the FastMCP Image type. Default to FLUX.2 klein-4b with q8 quantization.
acceptance: generate_image tool is registered with FastMCP and appears in tool listing

Tool accepts parameters: prompt (required), model (optional, default flux2-klein-4b), width (optional, default 1024), height (optional, default 1024), steps (optional, default 4), seed (optional, random), quantize (optional, default 8)
Tool calls mflux Python API (not subprocess) to generate the image
Tool returns the generated image via FastMCP Image type (base64)
Missing prompt returns a clear validation error
Invalid model name returns a descriptive error
Unit tests verify parameter validation (missing prompt, invalid dimensions, invalid quantize values)
Unit tests verify happy path with mocked model returning a mock PIL Image

title: edit_image MCP Tool
description: Implement the edit_image MCP tool for image-conditioned editing using FLUX.2's edit capability. The tool accepts image_paths, prompt, model, steps, and seed. It uses Flux2KleinEdit from the mflux Python API via the model cache.
acceptance: edit_image tool is registered with FastMCP and appears in tool listing
Tool accepts parameters: image_paths (required, list of file paths), prompt (required), model (optional), steps (optional, default 4), seed (optional)
Tool validates that all image_paths exist on disk before attempting generation
Tool calls mflux Flux2KleinEdit Python API to perform the edit
Tool returns the edited image via FastMCP Image type (base64)
Missing image_paths or prompt returns a clear validation error
Non-existent file path returns a descriptive error
Unit tests verify parameter validation and happy path with mocked model

title: list_models MCP Tool
description: Implement the list_models MCP tool that returns a structured list of all available mflux model families, their variants, capabilities, and recommended settings.
acceptance: list_models tool is registered with FastMCP and appears in tool listing
Tool returns a structured list including at minimum: FLUX.2, Z-Image, FIBO, FLUX.1, Qwen, SeedVR2
Each model entry includes: family name, variant names, description, supported features (edit, upscale, etc.), and recommended default steps
Unit test verifies the returned structure contains all expected model families

title: get_image_metadata MCP Tool
description: Implement the get_image_metadata MCP tool that reads and returns metadata embedded in mflux-generated images using mflux's MetadataReader.
acceptance: get_image_metadata tool is registered with FastMCP and appears in tool listing
Tool accepts an image_path parameter (required)
Tool calls mflux MetadataReader.read_all_metadata to extract metadata
Tool returns the metadata as a structured dict/JSON
Non-existent file path returns a descriptive error
File with no mflux metadata returns an empty result or descriptive message, not an exception
Unit tests verify happy path and error cases

title: CLI Transport Options
description: Add CLI argument parsing to server.py so it supports --transport (stdio or http) and --port flags. Default to stdio for local agent use. HTTP transport enables remote/networked usage.
acceptance: server.py accepts --transport flag with values 'stdio' (default) and 'http'
server.py accepts --port flag (default 8000) for HTTP transport
Running 'uv run server.py' starts in stdio mode
Running 'uv run server.py --transport http --port 8080' starts an HTTP server on port 8080
Invalid transport value produces a clear error message
Unit test or health check verifies the server starts without errors in stdio mode
